final_answer:
Because full Bor/Heimdall identifier renaming is not finished yet; only the safe runtime naming layer was changed.

Why this matters: you want a complete Gilt identity, and right now it is still mixed, which is not your final architecture.

Facts (confirmed):
- Binaries are renamed to `giltd` and `giltconsd`.
- Canonical testnet naming is now `gilttestnet`.
- Internal identifiers still exist in code/import paths (`bor`, `heimdall`, `github.com/0xPolygon/...`) to keep the code compiling with current dependency forks.
- The full namespace cutover (`github.com/giltchain/*`) is not completed.

Inference (likely cause):
- I kept compatibility-first internals to avoid immediate dependency/type breakage while stabilizing builds.

Actions (next steps):
1. Do a hard rename phase for all remaining `bor`/`heimdall` identifiers (code, package names, module paths, scripts, service names).
2. Fork and repoint the Cosmos/Comet/Heimdall dependency chain under your org first, then switch imports.
3. Run full rebuild/regression pass after that cutover.

Completion level: partial.  
Current blocker: dependency stack is still pinned to `0xPolygon` module/type paths, so full internal rename needs coordinated fork-and-repoint work.
